title: Pollinator Decline Detection And Crop-Pollination Service Risk In Agricultural Regions
summary: Build a verified workflow for estimating crop-pollination service risk from satellite-derived habitat-change indicators in LMIC agricultural regions, with explicit documentation of pollinator-monitoring data gaps and crop-pollination-dependence classification limitations.
why_it_matters: An estimated 5-8 percent of global crop production by volume depends on animal pollination, valued at $235-577 billion annually. Wild pollinator populations are declining in many regions, but over 80 percent of pollinator studies come from Europe and North America — the LMIC agricultural regions where pollinator-dependent crop production is growing fastest have almost no systematic wild pollinator monitoring. Without verified risk assessment, agricultural extension and conservation programs cannot target interventions where pollination-service loss would most affect food security and farmer livelihoods.

success_metrics.target: Every pollination-service risk claim documents whether direct pollinator monitoring data exists for the analyzed region or relies entirely on proxy indicators.
success_metrics.validation_method: Schema validation and reviewer spot-checks confirming that every regional claim states its evidence basis as direct monitoring, proxy, or assumption.

success_metrics.target: Every crop-pollination risk estimate documents the source and methodology of pollination-dependence classification, including whether it was derived from temperate or tropical studies.
success_metrics.validation_method: Reviewer confirms classification source, geographic applicability, and crop-specific confidence for each pollination-dependent crop analyzed.

success_metrics.target: No habitat-change-to-pollinator-abundance inference is merged without documenting the correlation basis and the absence of ground-truth pollinator monitoring in most LMIC regions.
success_metrics.validation_method: Red-team reviewer confirms every proxy-based claim has explicit limitations documenting what the proxy cannot prove about actual pollinator populations.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, proxy-validation safety review, and replication for quantitative outputs.
